ddls:
CREATE TABLE "instructions" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction */,
  "mnemonic" TEXT /* Mnemonic of the instruction (e.g., LSL, LSR, ROL) */,
  "operands" TEXT /* Operands required by the instruction (e.g., Rd) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the instruction */,
  "operation" TEXT /* Detailed operation performed by the instruction */,
  "flags_affected" TEXT /* Flags affected by the instruction (e.g., Z, C, N, V, H, S) */,
  "cycles" INTEGER /* Number of execution cycles required by the instruction */,
  "privilege_level" TEXT /* Privilege level of the instruction (e.g., user, kernel) */,
  "power_consumption" REAL /* Power consumption of the instruction (in watts) */,
  "pipelining_stages_count" INTEGER /* Number of pipelining stages involved in the instruction */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate of the instruction (e.g., 0.001) */,
  "average_cycles" INTEGER /* Average number of cycles required for the instruction */,
  "max_cycles" INTEGER /* Maximum number of cycles required for the instruction */,
  "min_cycles" INTEGER /* Minimum number of cycles required for the instruction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the instruction type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the instruction type */,
  "privilege_level" TEXT /* Privilege level of the instruction type (e.g., user, kernel) */,
  "performance_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the instruction type is performance-critical */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction category */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction type */,
  "performance_metrics_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_categories_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_categories_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES instruction_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_categories_performance_metrics_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_metrics_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operands" (
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operand */,
  "operand_name" TEXT /* Name of the operand (e.g., Rd) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operand */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the operand (e.g., integer, floating-point) */,
  "bit_width" INTEGER /* Bit width of the operand */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_operands" (
  "io_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction operand */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operand */,
  "operand_position" INTEGER /* Position of the operand in the instruction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("io_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_operands_operand_id FOREIGN KEY ("operand_id") REFERENCES operands ("operand_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_operands_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flags" (
  "flag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flag */,
  "flag_name" TEXT /* Name of the flag (e.g., Z, C, N, V, H, S) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the flag */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* Default reset value of the flag */,
  "writeable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the flag is writeable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_flags" (
  "if_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction flag */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "flag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the flag */,
  "read_only" INTEGER /* Indicates if the flag is read-only for the instruction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("if_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_flags_flag_id FOREIGN KEY ("flag_id") REFERENCES flags ("flag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_flags_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pipelining_stages" (
  "stage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pipelining stage */,
  "stage_name" TEXT /* Name of the pipelining stage */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the pipelining stage */,
  "stage_order" INTEGER /* Order of the pipelining stage */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency associated with the stage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_pipelining" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction pipelining stage */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "stage_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pipelining stage */,
  "stage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the instruction passes through the stage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_pipelining_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_pipelining_stage_id FOREIGN KEY ("stage_id") REFERENCES pipelining_stages ("stage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., execution time, IPC) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., seconds, cycles) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)
